claims.claim: Card view design guidelines recommend using a gallery grid with built-in CSS responsiveness — cards expand and compress at breakpoints rather than using masonry or bento layouts.
claims.quote: follow the grid layout, expanding and compressing at certain breakpoints

claims.claim: Card density toggle is supported via a toggle between card view and table/list view, not via a within-grid density slider.
claims.quote: A card view that toggles into a table or list view.

claims.claim: Card grids are recommended for data sets best identified by a graphic or visual representation, with cards serving as summaries that let users drill down for detail — directly applicable to mod browser information hierarchy.
claims.quote: Display items in a data set that are best identified by a graphic or other visual representation. Data set summaries, allowing users to drill down into any card to see more detailed content.

claims.claim: Card background treatment should match the page background: drop-shadow cards on gray backgrounds, flat cards on white backgrounds — a concrete card density/visual-weight recommendation.
claims.quote: Use the default border treatment (cards with drop shadows) for cards when placed on a gray background only. Flat cards should be used when placing cards on a white background.

claims.claim: At the smallest viewport breakpoint, cards expand to nearly the full screen width, meaning mobile layout collapses to a single-column list by default in the PatternFly grid system.
claims.quote: At smallest sizes, cards take up almost the entire width of the screen.
